This isn't like a confirmed ranking factor, but there are a lot of people also at GrowthX and just people who have been in SEO for a while who have observed that websites that have a lot of content, and when a lot of that content isn't performing that well, it sends a signal to Google that a lot of the content is irrelevant or unhelpful.
And that kind of makes it also harder for new content to rank.
So by pruning old content or content that isn't performing, we can kind of boost the performance of the overall blog and the glossary.
